title: Annual Trade Show Booth Setup Memo
title_zh: 年度商展攤位佈置備忘錄

all.en: This memo provides instructions for setting up our booth at the Annual Trade Show.
all.zh: 本備忘錄說明本公司於年度展會展位的佈置事宜。

all.en: The trade show will be held at the Northland Exhibition Hall from May 6 to May 9.
all.zh: 本展會將於 5 月 6 日至 5 月 9 日於 Northland 展覽館舉行。

all.en: Our booth is located in section C, space number 214.
all.zh: 本公司展位位於 C 區，編號 214。

all.en: The booth measures six meters wide by four meters deep.
all.zh: 展位寬六公尺、深四公尺。

all.en: Setup access begins at 8:00 a.m. on Tuesday, May 5.
all.zh: 佈展時間自 5 月 5 日星期二上午 8 點起開放。

all.en: All structures must be fully installed by 6:00 p.m. that same day.
all.zh: 所有結構物須於當日下午 6 點前完成架設。

all.en: The marketing team will arrive at 8:30 a.m. with the printed graphics.
all.zh: 行銷團隊將於上午 8 點半攜帶印製好的展板抵達現場。

all.en: The carpenters will assemble the backdrop wall during the first two hours.
all.zh: 木工人員將於前兩小時組裝背板牆面。

all.en: Electricians will install lighting and demo monitors by noon.
all.zh: 電工人員將於中午前完成燈光與示範螢幕的安裝。

all.en: All product samples should be unpacked and inspected before lunchtime.
all.zh: 所有產品樣品應於午餐前完成拆封與檢查。

all.en: Brochures and giveaways will be delivered directly to the booth on Tuesday morning.
all.zh: 型錄與贈品將於週二上午直接送達展位。

all.en: Please verify the quantities against the packing list upon arrival.
all.zh: 貨物送達時請依出貨單確認數量。

all.en: Any damaged items must be reported to the operations lead immediately.
all.zh: 如發現損壞物品，請立即通報營運負責人。

all.en: Booth staff training will take place from 4:00 to 5:30 p.m. on May 5.
all.zh: 展位人員訓練將於 5 月 5 日下午 4 點至 5 點半進行。

all.en: Daily booth hours are from 9:30 a.m. to 5:30 p.m.
all.zh: 每日展位開放時間為上午 9 點半至下午 5 點半。

all.en: Staff should arrive at least thirty minutes before opening each day.
all.zh: 工作人員每日應於開展前至少三十分鐘抵達。

all.en: Two team members must remain at the booth at all times during open hours.
all.zh: 開展期間展位內隨時應有兩名以上人員留守。

all.en: All staff are required to wear the company polo shirt and name badge.
all.zh: 所有人員須穿著公司 Polo 衫並配戴名牌。

all.en: Visitors may scan our QR code to request a follow-up consultation.
all.zh: 訪客可掃描 QR 碼以申請後續諮詢服務。

all.en: Daily lead reports must be submitted by 7:00 p.m. each evening.
all.zh: 每日商機回報須於晚間 7 點前提交。

all.en: Teardown will begin at 5:30 p.m. on Saturday, May 9.
all.zh: 撤展作業將於 5 月 9 日星期六下午 5 點半開始。

all.en: All equipment must be packed and removed by 10:00 p.m. that night.
all.zh: 所有設備須於當晚 10 點前完成打包並撤離。

all.en: If you have any questions, please contact our event coordinator.
all.zh: 如有任何問題，請聯絡本公司活動專案窗口。

all.en: Thank you in advance for your cooperation and professionalism.
all.zh: 預先感謝各位的配合與專業表現。

all.en: A daily morning briefing will be held at the booth at 9:00 a.m. each day.
all.zh: 每日上午 9 點將於展位舉行晨間簡報。

all.en: Lead capture tablets will be pre-configured with our latest sales materials.
all.zh: 名單蒐集平板將預先載入最新的銷售資料。

all.en: Refreshments for staff will be available in the exhibitor lounge throughout the show.
all.zh: 展會期間參展者休息室將為工作人員提供茶點。

all.en: Press kits should be reserved for journalists and prominent industry visitors.
all.zh: 新聞媒體資料袋應保留給記者及重要產業訪客。

all.en: Our keynote presentation is scheduled for Wednesday at 2:00 p.m. in Hall B.
all.zh: 本公司主題簡報訂於週三下午 2 點於 B 廳舉行。

all.en: Booth photography is permitted, but please avoid filming competitor products.
all.zh: 展位內可拍照，但請勿拍攝競爭對手產品。

all.en: Emergency contact numbers for venue staff are posted near the booth entrance.
all.zh: 場館工作人員緊急聯絡電話張貼於展位入口附近。

all.en: All visitor inquiries that require pricing details should be forwarded to the sales lead.
all.zh: 所有涉及報價細節的訪客詢問應轉介給業務主導。

all.en: A debrief meeting will be conducted at our office on Monday following the trade show.
all.zh: 展會結束後的下週一將於辦公室召開檢討會議。

all.en: We appreciate everyone's hard work and look forward to a successful event.
all.zh: 感謝大家辛勤付出，期待這次展會圓滿成功。

all.en: Security passes for booth staff must be picked up at the registration desk on Tuesday.
all.zh: 展位人員的安全證件須於週二至報到櫃台領取。

all.en: Personal belongings should be stored in the locked storage closet behind the backdrop.
all.zh: 個人物品應存放於背板後方的上鎖儲物櫃內。

all.en: Shuttle service from the partner hotel will run every twenty minutes during show hours.
all.zh: 展會期間合作飯店每二十分鐘將提供一班接駁車。

all.en: Booth staff rotations are scheduled to ensure adequate breaks throughout each day.
all.zh: 展位人員輪值安排將確保每日有充足休息時間。

all.en: Trade show organizers will conduct a safety briefing on Tuesday at three in the afternoon.
all.zh: 展會主辦單位將於週二下午三點舉行安全簡報。

all.en: All marketing collateral must be approved by the brand team before being displayed.
all.zh: 所有行銷文宣須經品牌團隊核准後方可展示。

all.en: Translators will be available at the booth to support international visitors each afternoon.
all.zh: 每日下午展位將安排口譯人員以協助國際訪客。

all.en: Live product demonstrations will run every hour on the hour from ten to four.
all.zh: 現場產品示範將於每日上午十點至下午四點整點進行。

all.en: Photography of the booth setup is welcome before the opening ceremony on Wednesday morning.
all.zh: 週三上午開幕典禮前歡迎拍攝展位佈置照片。

all.en: We are confident this trade show will generate strong leads and brand visibility.
all.zh: 我們深信此次展會將帶來大量商機並提升品牌能見度。

all.en: I forgot to pack comfortable shoes for the trade show, and by day two my feet were honestly destroyed.
all.zh: 我忘記帶舒服的鞋子去展會，到第二天我的腳真的快不行了。

all.en: My colleague accidentally gave away our last brochure to a journalist, who fortunately found it very useful later.
all.zh: 我同事不小心把最後一本型錄給了記者，所幸對方後來覺得超實用。

all.en: Have you ever stood at a trade show booth for nine hours straight and lost your voice by the closing day?
all.zh: 你有沒有試過在展位連站九個小時，到最後一天聲音都啞掉？

all.en: Honestly, the competitor across the aisle had a coffee machine, and that alone drew twice the foot traffic.
all.zh: 說真的，對面競爭對手的展位放了一台咖啡機，光是這樣人潮就多了一倍。

all.en: My manager kept rearranging the product samples every two hours because she could not decide on the right layout.
all.zh: 我主管每兩個小時就重新排一次產品樣品，因為她一直決定不了該怎麼擺。

all.en: I once spilled coffee on the printed banner moments before the doors opened, and the carpenter saved me with tape.
all.zh: 我有一次開展前幾分鐘把咖啡灑到展板上，木工人員拿膠帶救了我一命。

all.en: My boss promised pizza for the team after teardown, and that promise alone kept everyone going until midnight.
all.zh: 我老闆答應撤展後請大家吃披薩，光憑這個承諾大家撐到半夜都沒問題。

all.en: Honestly, the best leads usually come from random conversations in the lunch line, not from the booth itself.
all.zh: 老實說，最棒的商機常常是排午餐時隨口聊出來的，不是在展位上。

all.en: I always bring snacks to hide behind the backdrop, because hotel breakfast feels like a distant memory by 3 p.m.
all.zh: 我每次都會帶零食藏在背板後面，因為下午三點時飯店早餐已經像遠古記憶了。

all.en: Just a tip: scan visitor badges immediately, because trying to remember which person said what is genuinely impossible later.
all.zh: 小提醒：訪客來時立刻掃他們的證件，因為事後想記得誰說過什麼根本不可能。
